cases.include_reason: Career offer choice with negotiation, family constraints, equity/EV ambiguity, and short deadline. Useful for testing whether v60 adds BATNA/optionality/trade-off discipline without model theater.

nominations.reason: Explicit probe to keep a medium-confidence fallback/walk-away card visible in a negotiation-heavy case.

risk_notes: Synthetic corpus case; do not treat as product-readiness evidence.
Can overfit toward business/career advice if the prompt rewards familiar consulting structure.

cases.include_reason: Legal/ethical high-stakes reporting case with power asymmetry, retaliation risk, evidence threshold, and sequencing pressure.
risk_notes: High-stakes case; output must not become legal advice or invent protected facts.
Good v60 behavior may defer several cards because key evidence is missing.

cases.include_reason: Short strategic pivot case with known extraction fabrication pressure. Useful for testing whether absence and evidence requirements reduce thin-signal overconfidence.
risk_notes: Known prior fabrication pattern; must distinguish source-thinness from system failure.
Do not reward confident pivot advice unless case evidence supports it.

cases.include_reason: Sparse personal/financial decision under time pressure. Useful for absence-first behavior and weak/medium support warnings.

nominations.reason: Explicit weak-support probe; included to test warning visibility and rejection/defer discipline, not because the case necessarily activates it.

risk_notes: Sparse facts can make generic financial advice look better than it is.
A good C-arm may ask narrower questions rather than change the recommendation.

cases.include_reason: Personal/interpersonal money dilemma that originally sat near the old strategic-gate boundary. Useful for checking that v60 does not overfit to business-shaped decisions.
risk_notes: Personal stakes can be distorted by corporate negotiation frames.
Good output may preserve relationship nuance rather than optimize only for leverage.

cases.include_reason: Messy multi-thread conversation with known live-constraint instability. Useful for testing whether grouped cards separate decisions or amplify confusion.
risk_notes: Topic jumping can make any packet look comprehensive while being incoherent.
Good output should separate decisions before applying pressure.

cases.include_reason: Narrow case where the user already has a plan. Useful as a control for whether v60 adds real pressure or merely lengthens crisp advice.
risk_notes: Do not reward extra complexity if the baseline answer is already adequate.
Zero visible card use may be a correct outcome.

cases.include_reason: Long professional uncertainty case with evolving context and dense constraints. Useful for cap pressure, broad/meta-card burden, and stability across a longer transcript.
risk_notes: Longer transcript can make broad cards tempting.
Good output should prioritize decision structure rather than dump all plausible lenses.
